hypothesis: If an EGNN-based diffusion model (AMDEN) with VE/VP noise schedules, ghost-atom density control, and an energy-based denoiser with HMC refinement is trained on amorphous datasets, then it will generate structures whose RDFs, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and potential energy distributions match MD references across thermal histories and, when conditioned on Young’s modulus, achieve target moduli.
